zh.title: Solana/美元技术分析：震荡偏强整理
zh.content:
当前SOL价格围绕64.88附近运行，处于近期高位震荡区间，整体呈现震荡偏强结构，多头仍占优但动能有限，短期维持盘整态势

MACD维持0.46上方运行，动能柱略偏多，说明短线仍有上行动力。价格站稳SMA20上方，均线结构偏多排列，延续偏强格局，短线交易活跃

当前震荡区间上沿65.2形成短期阻力，下方64.6为即时支撑，若跌破将测试63.8均线支撑位，区间内高抛低吸为主

操作上建议维持回调做多思路，突破65.2可顺势加仓，若跌破64.6则需控制风险并等待低吸机会，整体保持偏多结构运行

en.title: Solana vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
SOL/USD is trading at 64.88, holding above its 20-day simple moving average at 63.88, which suggests the broader short-term trend remains constructive despite the flat 24-hour performance. Price action has been relatively compressed between 64.61 and 65.22, indicating a period of consolidation after recent gains. As long as the market maintains closes above the 20-day average, buyers retain a modest technical advantage and the prevailing bias remains cautiously bullish.

Momentum indicators provide a mixed but generally supportive picture. While RSI data is unavailable, the positive MACD reading of 0.4678 indicates bullish momentum remains present beneath the surface. The absence of a signal-line value limits confirmation strength, but a positive MACD typically reflects upward price pressure and improving trend conditions. Traders should monitor whether momentum continues to expand, as sustained positive MACD readings often precede further upside attempts.

From a key levels perspective, immediate support is located near 64.60, corresponding to the recent session low. A secondary support zone emerges around the 20-day SMA near 63.88, which could attract buyers on pullbacks. On the upside, resistance is evident around 65.20–65.25, the recent intraday high. A decisive breakout above this area could open the path toward the next psychological resistance region near 66.00, while failure to hold support may trigger a retracement toward the low-63 range.

For trading purposes, trend-following participants may favor maintaining a bullish stance while price remains above 63.88. Conservative traders could wait for a confirmed break above 65.25 before considering fresh long positions, targeting higher resistance levels. Alternatively, pullbacks toward support may present lower-risk entry opportunities if accompanied by stabilizing price action. Risk management remains essential, with protective stops placed below key support zones to limit downside exposure should momentum weaken unexpectedly.
